info.name: Social Event Mapper API
info.description: Generated from docs/openapi/*.yaml — run docs/postman/generate_from_openapi.py to refresh.

variable.description: Hostname only; path includes the `api` segment. Local example: localhost:8080

item.name: Admin
item.description:
Web-only admin backoffice APIs for read tables and privileged mutations.

All endpoints require a bearer access token whose `role` claim is `ADMIN`.
Anonymous callers receive `401`; authenticated non-admin callers receive `403`.

request.description:
operationId: `adminListUsers`

List users

operationId: `adminDeactivateUser`

Deactivate a user account

Sets the account status to `deactivated`, revokes active sessions and push
devices, cancels hosted active/in-progress events, cancels active/pending
non-host participations and linked tickets, and cancels pending invitations
and join requests involving the user. Repeating the request is idempotent.

operationId: `adminListEvents`

List events

operationId: `adminUpdateEventStatus`

Change event status

operationId: `adminCancelEvent`

Cancel an event

Cancels the event plus active/pending participations, linked tickets, pending invitations, and pending join requests in one application transaction.

operationId: `adminListEventReports`

List event reports

operationId: `adminUpdateEventReportStatus`

Change event report moderation status

operationId: `adminListCategories`

List event categories

operationId: `adminCreateCategory`

Create event category

operationId: `adminDeleteCategory`

Delete event category

Returns `409 category_in_use` when any event still references the category.

operationId: `adminListParticipations`

List participations

operationId: `adminCreateParticipation`

Create a manual participation

Creates or reactivates a manual participation for the selected event/user pair.
`status` defaults to `APPROVED`. Only `APPROVED` and `PENDING` are accepted.

The event and user must exist. If the user already has an active
`APPROVED` or `PENDING` participation for the event, the API returns `409`.
For `PROTECTED` events, creating an `APPROVED` participation also creates
or reactivates the linked `ACTIVE` ticket in the same application mutation.
Event participant counts are maintained by the existing participation count triggers.

operationId: `adminCancelParticipation`

Cancel a participation

Marks the participation `CANCELED` and cancels linked `ACTIVE` or `PENDING`
tickets in the same application mutation. If the participation is already
`CANCELED`, the endpoint is idempotent: it returns `200` with
`already_canceled: true` and does not perform another ticket mutation.

operationId: `adminListTickets`

List tickets

operationId: `adminListInvitations`

List invitations

operationId: `adminUpdateInvitationStatus`

Change invitation status

Admins may cancel pending invitations. Non-pending rows return a conflict.

operationId: `adminListJoinRequests`

List join requests

operationId: `adminUpdateJoinRequestStatus`

Change join request status

Admins may cancel or reject pending join requests. Non-pending rows return a conflict.

operationId: `adminListComments`

List discussion and review comments

operationId: `adminDeleteComment`

Delete a comment

Hard-deletes the comment because the current schema has no comment soft-delete status.

operationId: `adminListEventRatings`

List event ratings

operationId: `adminDeleteEventRating`

Delete an event rating

operationId: `adminListParticipantRatings`

List participant ratings

operationId: `adminDeleteParticipantRating`

Delete a participant rating

operationId: `adminListFavoriteEvents`

List favorite events

operationId: `adminListFavoriteLocations`

List favorite locations

operationId: `adminListUserBadges`

List user badges

operationId: `adminListPushDevices`

List push devices

operationId: `adminRevokePushDevice`

Revoke a push device

operationId: `adminListNotifications`

List sent notifications

Lists notification inbox rows for backoffice review, including delivery-attempt summary counts.

operationId: `adminCreateNotification`

Send a custom notification

Sends a custom notification to explicit user IDs selected by an admin.
`delivery_mode` controls delivery attempts: `IN_APP` creates inbox rows
and attempts SSE delivery, `PUSH` creates inbox rows and attempts push
delivery to active devices, and `BOTH` attempts both SSE and push.

The API does not log notification body text or other sensitive free-form
content. `idempotency_key` is optional; when supplied, duplicate calls with
the same key for the same receiver return existing rows and increment
`idempotent_count`.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints for phase-one user registration, password-reset OTP requests,
and session management.

Registration uses email OTP only. Phone number is optional and is stored without
mandatory verification. Login uses username and password without an OTP step.

Session behavior for frontend clients:
- Access tokens live for 15 minutes.
- Refresh tokens are rotated on every successful `/auth/refresh` call.
- Each newly issued refresh token lives for up to 14 days.
- The full refresh-token family has a 60-day absolute maximum lifetime from the
  original login or registration session start.
- After a successful refresh, the previous refresh token must be discarded by the client.

OTP delivery uses the backend's configured transactional mail provider. Local and
dev defaults use Resend with single-recipient sends.

The `access_token` returned by successful sign-in and refresh responses is the same
bearer token used by protected APIs such as the Event API under the `/api` server.

item.name: Request an email OTP for registration

request.description:
operationId: `requestRegistrationEmailOtp`

Request an email OTP for registration

Submit an email address to start the registration flow. The response stays intentionally generic to reduce account enumeration.

operationId: `requestPasswordResetOtp`

Request a password-reset OTP

Submit an email address to start the forgot-password flow.

The response is intentionally generic and always returns `200 OK` for valid requests,
whether or not an account exists for the email address. Unknown emails, resend cooldowns,
and request throttling are all handled silently to reduce account enumeration and OTP abuse.

operationId: `verifyPasswordResetOtp`

Verify a password-reset OTP

Validate the six-digit OTP sent during the forgot-password request flow.

On success, the backend returns a short-lived `reset_token`. The client must
submit that token to `/auth/forgot-password/reset-password` together with the
email address and the new password.

item.name: Reset the password after OTP verification

request.description:
operationId: `resetForgotPassword`

Reset the password after OTP verification

Replace the account password using the short-lived `reset_token` issued by
`/auth/forgot-password/verify-otp`.

The `reset_token` is single-use and expires automatically. After a successful
password reset, the previous password can no longer be used for login.

item.name: Check whether registration username and email are available

request.description:
operationId: `checkRegistrationAvailability`

Check whether registration username and email are available

Validate the registration identity fields before OTP verification and return whether each value is
currently available for a new account.

This endpoint intentionally returns explicit availability results for `username` and `email` to support
client-side registration flows. To reduce automated enumeration, the endpoint is rate-limited per client.

item.name: Verify an email OTP and create the account

request.description:
operationId: `verifyRegistrationEmailOtp`

Verify an email OTP and create the account

Complete registration by submitting the received OTP and the user's initial credentials.

operationId: `loginWithPassword`

Authenticate with username and password

Create a new session by exchanging a username and password for access and refresh tokens.

The response contains:
- a 15-minute access token for authenticated API calls
- a refresh token that can be exchanged for a new session for up to 14 days

Refresh tokens are rotated. If the client later calls `/auth/refresh`, it must replace
the stored refresh token with the newly returned one.

item.name: Rotate a refresh token and issue a new session

request.description:
operationId: `refreshSession`

Rotate a refresh token and issue a new session

Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access token and a rotated refresh token.

Important client behavior:
- Every successful refresh invalidates the previously used refresh token.
- The client must persist the newly returned refresh token and stop using the old one.
- Each rotated refresh token lives for up to 14 days from when it is issued.
- The refresh-token family still has a 60-day absolute maximum lifetime from the
  original session start, even if refresh calls continue to succeed before then.

Once the absolute session limit is reached, the user must log in again.

operationId: `logoutSession`

Revoke a refresh token

Invalidate a refresh token so it can no longer be used to mint new sessions.

Mobile clients may include `device_installation_id` to revoke the current
app installation's active FCM token assignment at the same time.

item.name: Badge
item.description: Authenticated endpoints for browsing the badge catalog and listing badges earned by the authenticated user or another public profile owner. Badge state is recomputed synchronously after participation, rating, and favorite-location writes; clients should refetch these endpoints when they expect the user's badge list to have changed.

item.name: List the authenticated user's earned badges

request.description:
List the authenticated user's earned badges

Returns the badges earned by the authenticated user, ordered by `earned_at` descending. The response shape is identical to `GET /users/{id}/badges`.

item.name: List a public profile's earned badges

request.description:
List a public profile's earned badges

Returns the badges earned by the public profile owner identified by `id`. Unlike `/me/badges`, this endpoint never returns viewer-specific progress data and is safe to call with any user id.

List the full badge catalog

Returns every badge defined in the catalog (ordered by `sort_order` ascending) annotated with whether the authenticated viewer has earned each one. Unearned badges have `earned=false` and `earned_at=null`.

item.name: Categories
item.description:
Public read-only endpoint for listing available event categories.

No authentication is required. Use the returned `id` values as `category_id`
when creating events or as `category_ids` filter values when discovering events.

request.description:
operationId: `listCategories`

List all event categories

Returns all available event categories ordered by `id` ascending.

The default database seed provides 20 categories (ids `1`–`20`).
This endpoint requires no authentication and is safe to call before login.

item.name: Events
item.description:
Authenticated event-management endpoints for creating and managing social events.

Obtain an access token from the Auth API (`POST /auth/login` or
`POST /auth/register/email/verify`) and send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
when calling protected operations in this definition.

Behavioral notes for create-event clients:
- The authenticated user becomes the event host.
- A host can create at most one event with a given `title`. Reusing the same trimmed title for the same host returns `409 Conflict`.
- Tags and constraints are persisted in the same transaction as the event.
- Enum-like fields are case-sensitive and must be sent in uppercase.
- `start_time` and `end_time` must be RFC 3339 date-times with a timezone designator such as `Z` or `+03:00`.
- Each event has **exactly one** category: send a single `category_id` (not a list). It must reference an existing row in `event_category`. The default database seed defines twenty categories (ids `1`–`20`); see `CreateEventRequestBase.category_id` for the id-to-name map.

request.description:
operationId: `updateEvent`

Update an active event

Allows the authenticated host to edit an `ACTIVE` event before it starts.
The request is partial: omitted fields are unchanged, while explicit `null`
clears nullable fields such as `description`, `category_id`, `address`,
`end_time`, and `capacity`.

Reconfirmation-triggering changes are `title`, `description`, `category_id`,
location/address/geometry/route changes, `start_time`, `end_time`, and
adding a new constraint. When any of those changes occur, all currently
`APPROVED` non-host participations become `PENDING`, their active tickets
become `PENDING`, and participants receive an `EVENT_RECONFIRMATION_REQUIRED`
notification with deep link `/events/{id}`. Users already in `PENDING` are
not notified.

`constraints`, when present, replaces the full constraint list. Removing
constraints alone does not require reconfirmation. `capacity` cannot be set
below `approved_participant_count + pending_participant_count`.

variable.description: UUID of the event to fetch.

request.description:
operationId: `getEventDetail`

Get event detail

Returns the full event-detail payload for the caller. Authentication is optional —
both unauthenticated and authenticated requests are accepted.

Visibility rules:
- `PUBLIC` and `PROTECTED` events are readable by any caller, authenticated or not.
- `PRIVATE` events are readable only by the host, users with an `APPROVED`
  participation, users with an `ACCEPTED` invitation, or users with a `PENDING`
  invitation (so invited users can review the event before responding). Anonymous
  callers cannot read `PRIVATE` events.
- When a `PRIVATE` event is not readable by the caller, the API responds with
  `404 event_not_found` to avoid leaking the event's existence.

Response notes:
- `status` is returned exactly as stored on the event row; canceled and completed
  events remain readable.
- `host_score` returns the host's cached aggregate rating summary used by discovery cards.
- `rating_window` describes the event-specific 7-day mutation window computed from `COALESCE(end_time, start_time)`.
- `viewer_event_rating` returns the authenticated participant's current rating for the event, if one exists.
- `viewer_context` is always present. For unauthenticated callers, `participation_status`
  is `null`, `join_request_status` is `null`, and `is_favorited` is `false`.
- `viewer_context.participation_status` contains only real participation rows:
  `APPROVED`, `PENDING`, `LEAVED`, `CANCELED`, or `null`. Pending join requests
  are exposed separately as `viewer_context.join_request_status`.
- When an accepted participant must reconfirm edited event details,
  `viewer_context.needs_reconfirmation` is `true` and `viewer_context.event_diff`
  compares the user's `last_confirmed_event_version` with the latest event version.
- Responses are viewer-specific when a bearer token is supplied, so the endpoint returns
  `Cache-Control: private, no-store` and `Vary: Authorization`.
- Host management collections are no longer embedded in this response. Hosts should use
  `GET /events/{id}/host-context`, `GET /events/{id}/participants`,
  `GET /events/{id}/join-requests`, and `GET /events/{id}/invitations`.

operationId: `getEventHostContextSummary`

Get host management summary

Returns host-only management counters for the event without loading the underlying
participant, join-request, or invitation collections.

item.name: Reconfirm participation after event details changed

request.description:
operationId: `reconfirmEventParticipation`

Reconfirm participation after event details changed

Allows an authenticated participant with `PENDING` participation status to
reconfirm attendance for the latest event details. On success the
participation becomes `APPROVED`, `reconfirmed_at` and
`last_confirmed_event_version` are updated, and the participant's ticket,
including an existing `PENDING` ticket created by an event edit, is
reactivated as `ACTIVE`.

item.name: List approved participants for a hosted event

request.description:
operationId: `listEventApprovedParticipants`

List approved participants for a hosted event

Returns a cursor-paginated list of approved participants visible only to the event host.

item.name: List pending join requests for a hosted event

request.description:
operationId: `listEventPendingJoinRequests`

List pending join requests for a hosted event

Returns a cursor-paginated list of pending join requests visible only to the event host.

item.name: List invitations for a hosted event

request.description:
operationId: `listEventInvitations`

List invitations for a hosted event

Returns a cursor-paginated list of invitations visible only to the event host.

item.name: Invite users to a private event

request.description:
operationId: `createEventInvitations`

Invite users to a private event

Allows the authenticated event host to invite up to 100 users by exact username.
This endpoint is supported only for `PRIVATE` events. The request is partial-success
for per-username business outcomes: unknown or syntactically invalid usernames are
returned in `invalid_usernames`, and known but ineligible usernames are returned in
`failed` with a stable reason code. System errors still fail the whole request.
Each successfully created invitation creates a notification for the invited user with
the event image URL when present. Notification delivery failures do not fail the
invitation batch.

item.name: Create direct-upload URLs for an event image

variable.description: UUID of the event whose image will be uploaded.

request.description:
operationId: `createEventImageUploadUrl`

Create direct-upload URLs for an event image

Preferred event-image flow:
1. Create the event first with `POST /events`.
2. Call this endpoint as the event host to receive presigned `PUT` uploads for `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL`.
3. Upload both client-generated JPEG files directly to DigitalOcean Spaces.
4. Call `POST /events/{id}/image/confirm` with the returned `confirm_token`.

Client rule:
- Forward every returned upload header exactly as returned, including `x-amz-acl: public-read`.

Naming convention:
- original object key: `events/{event_id}/cover/v{n}-{upload_id}`
- small object key: `events/{event_id}/cover/v{n}-{upload_id}-small`

Persistence and cache behavior:
- The backend stores the returned `base_url` in `event.image_url`.
- Clients can fetch the small variant by appending `-small` to the stored `base_url`.
- Existing files are never deleted. Each confirmed upload increments `image_version`, leaving older CDN URLs cache-safe.
- This flow remains compatible with limited-access Spaces keys because each uploaded object is explicitly written with the `public-read` canned ACL.

variable.description: UUID of the event whose image upload will be confirmed.

request.description:
operationId: `confirmEventImageUpload`

Confirm an uploaded event image

Confirms a previously presigned event image upload after both `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL` files have been uploaded successfully. The request body contains only the opaque `confirm_token`; clients do not send object URLs back. The confirmed CDN URL is readable without a signed GET URL because the upload step uses `x-amz-acl: public-read`.

item.name: List event discussion and review comments

query.description: Opaque cursor for the next discussion page.
Opaque cursor for the next review page.

request.description:
operationId: `listEventComments`

List event discussion and review comments

Returns top-level comments for a PUBLIC or PROTECTED event. Authentication is optional.

The response always contains two independently paginated collections:
`discussion_comments` and `review_comments`, both ordered by latest comment first.
Child comments are not embedded; use `GET /events/{id}/comments/{commentId}/replies`
when the user chooses to see replies. Reply collections are ordered by oldest reply first
so threaded conversations read chronologically.

PRIVATE events do not expose comments. If the authenticated caller can otherwise see
the private event, the API returns `409 comments_not_allowed`; if not visible, it returns
`404 event_not_found`.

item.name: Create a discussion comment or reply

request.description:
operationId: `createEventDiscussionComment`

Create a discussion comment or reply

Creates a discussion comment on a PUBLIC or PROTECTED event.

Rules:
- `ACTIVE`: any authenticated user may comment or reply.
- `IN_PROGRESS`: only the host or a qualifying participant may comment or reply.
- `COMPLETED`: discussion writes are rejected; participants should use review comments.
- `CANCELED`: all comment writes are rejected.
- Replies must target a top-level DISCUSSION comment from the same event.

item.name: List replies for a discussion comment

request.description:
operationId: `listEventCommentReplies`

List replies for a discussion comment

Returns replies for a top-level DISCUSSION comment, ordered oldest first.

item.name: Create or update the caller's completed-event review comment

request.description:
operationId: `upsertEventReviewComment`

Create or update the caller's completed-event review comment

Creates or updates the authenticated participant's review for a completed PUBLIC or
PROTECTED event. Reviews are singular per `(event_id, user_id)` and are the canonical
source for participant-to-event ratings. The optional `image_confirm_token` is returned
by `POST /events/{id}/review-comments/image/upload-url` after the client uploads both
image variants.

item.name: Create upload URLs for a review image

request.description:
operationId: `createEventReviewImageUpload`

Create upload URLs for a review image

Creates presigned upload instructions for the authenticated participant's one review image.
The caller must upload both `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL` JPEG variants, then pass the returned
`confirm_token` as `image_confirm_token` when upserting the review comment.

item.name: Create upload URLs for a join-request image

variable.description: UUID of the protected event the caller wants to join.

request.description:
operationId: `createEventJoinRequestImageUpload`

Create upload URLs for a join-request image

Creates presigned upload instructions for the authenticated user's optional one image on a protected-event join request.
The caller must upload both `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL` JPEG variants, then pass the returned
`confirm_token` as `image_confirm_token` when calling `POST /events/{id}/join-request`.

Naming convention:
- original object key: `events/{event_id}/join-requests/{user_id}/{upload_id}`
- small object key: `events/{event_id}/join-requests/{user_id}/{upload_id}-small`

operationId: `createEventReport`

Report an event

Creates a moderation report for the event from the authenticated user.

Client flow:
- Always require the user to choose `report_category` and enter a non-blank `message`.
- To attach an image, first call `POST /events/{id}/reports/image/upload-url`, upload both JPEG variants,
  then send the returned `confirm_token` as `image_confirm_token`.
- Report images are accepted only when the event's computed status is `IN_PROGRESS` or `COMPLETED`.
- Reports are stored with status `PENDING` for future moderation workflows.

item.name: Create upload URLs for a report image

request.description:
operationId: `createEventReportImageUpload`

Create upload URLs for a report image

Creates presigned upload instructions for an optional event-report image.
The authenticated caller must upload both `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL` JPEG variants,
then pass the returned `confirm_token` as `image_confirm_token` when creating the report.

This endpoint returns `409 event_report_image_not_allowed` unless the event's computed status
is `IN_PROGRESS` or `COMPLETED`.

item.name: Create or update the caller's event rating

request.description:
operationId: `upsertEventRating`

Create or update the caller's event rating

Deprecated compatibility endpoint. Creates or updates the authenticated participant's
completed-event REVIEW comment without an image. New clients should use
`POST /events/{id}/review-comments`.

Rules:
- The authenticated user must be a qualifying participant of the event.
- The event host cannot rate their own event, even though hosts have an internal approved participation row.
- Reviews are allowed only after the event status is `COMPLETED`.
- `CANCELED` and `PRIVATE` events cannot be reviewed.
- The resource is singular per `(user_id, event_id)`, so repeated `PUT` requests update the same review comment.

operationId: `deleteEventRating`

Delete the caller's event rating

Deprecated compatibility endpoint. Hard deletes the authenticated participant's
REVIEW comment/rating for the event. New clients should manage reviews through
the comments surface.

item.name: Create or update a host rating for a participant

request.description:
operationId: `upsertParticipantRating`

Create or update a host rating for a participant

Creates or updates the authenticated host's rating for an approved participant of the event.

Rules:
- The authenticated user must host the event.
- The target user must be an `APPROVED` participant of the same event.
- Hosts cannot rate themselves.
- Ratings are allowed only within the 7-day window computed from `COALESCE(end_time, start_time)`.
- `CANCELED` events cannot be rated.

item.name: Delete a host rating for a participant

request.description:
operationId: `deleteParticipantRating`

Delete a host rating for a participant

Hard deletes the authenticated host's rating for an approved participant of the event.
The same authorization and rating-window rules used by `PUT /events/{id}/participants/{participantUserId}/rating` also apply here.

variable.description: UUID of the event to join.

request.description:
operationId: `joinEvent`

Join a public event

Directly joins a **PUBLIC** event. Creates an APPROVED participation record for the
authenticated user and provisions an `ACTIVE` ticket for event entry flows.

Rules:
- The event must exist and have `privacy_level: PUBLIC`.
- The authenticated user must not be the event host.
- A user can only join the same event once; a second attempt returns `409`.
- If the user previously left before `start_time`, the backend reactivates the same participation row as `APPROVED`.
- If the user left at or after `start_time`, later direct join attempts return `409 already_participating`.
- Calling this endpoint on a `PROTECTED` event returns `409 event_join_not_allowed`.

variable.description: UUID of the event to leave.

request.description:
operationId: `leaveEvent`

Leave an event

Marks the authenticated user's participation as `LEAVED`.

Rules:
- The event must exist.
- The authenticated user must currently have an `APPROVED` participation.
- The event host cannot leave their own event.
- A pending join request does not qualify; only approved participants may leave.
- Leaving is allowed before `start_time` and while the event is still active.
- Leaving is no longer allowed after the event has ended or once the event is already `CANCELED` or `COMPLETED`.
- If the user leaves before `start_time`, the event is excluded from completed/past participation lists.
- If the user leaves before `start_time`, they can join again directly on `PUBLIC` events or submit a new join request on `PROTECTED` events.
- If the user leaves after `start_time`, completed/past participation lists may still include the event after it finishes.
- If the user leaves at or after `start_time`, they cannot join the same event again.

operationId: `cancelEvent`

Cancel an event

Transitions an **ACTIVE** event to **CANCELED**. Only the event host may perform this action.

Rules:
- The event must exist and have `status: ACTIVE`.
- Only the authenticated host can cancel their own event.
- Canceled events are excluded from discovery results but remain accessible via the detail endpoint.

operationId: `completeEvent`

Mark event as completed (host)

Transitions an **ACTIVE** or **IN_PROGRESS** event to **COMPLETED**. Only the event host may perform this action.

Use this to close **open-ended** events (no `end_time`) or to finish a timed event early. The background job may also complete events when `end_time` has passed.

Rules:
- The event must exist.
- Only the authenticated host can complete their own event.
- If the event is already **CANCELED** or **COMPLETED**, the request returns **409**.

item.name: Request to join a protected event

variable.description: UUID of the event to request joining.

request.description:
operationId: `requestJoinEvent`

Request to join a protected event

Submits a join request for a **PROTECTED** event. Creates a `join_request`
row with `status: PENDING` awaiting host approval.

Rules:
- The event must exist and have `privacy_level: PROTECTED`.
- The authenticated user must not be the event host.
- A user can only submit one pending request per event; a second attempt returns `409`.
- After the host rejects a request, the same user must wait **3 days** before requesting again.
- When the cooldown expires, the backend reactivates the same `join_request` row as `PENDING`.
- When a reactivated request omits `image_confirm_token`, any previous join-request image is cleared.
- If the user previously had an approved participation but left before `start_time`, the backend reactivates the same `join_request` row as `PENDING` so the host can approve them again.
- If the user left at or after `start_time`, later request attempts return `409 already_participating`.
- Calling this endpoint on a `PUBLIC` event returns `409 event_join_not_allowed`.

variable.description: UUID of the event being moderated.
UUID of the join request to approve.

request.description:
operationId: `approveJoinRequest`

Approve a pending join request

Allows the authenticated event host to approve a **PENDING** join request for a
**PROTECTED** event. Approval updates the join request to `APPROVED` and creates an
`APPROVED` participation row atomically. If the requester had left before
`start_time`, approval reactivates the same participation row instead of creating a
second one. After success, the requester receives an event notification with the
event image URL when present; notification delivery failures do not fail approval.

variable.description: UUID of the event being moderated.
UUID of the join request to reject.

request.description:
operationId: `rejectJoinRequest`

Reject a pending join request

Allows the authenticated event host to reject a **PENDING** join request for a
**PROTECTED** event. Rejection updates the join request to `REJECTED` and starts a
**3-day** re-request cooldown for the same user and event. After success, the
requester receives an event notification with the event image URL when present;
notification delivery failures do not fail rejection.

query.description: Latitude of the search origin in WGS 84.
Longitude of the search origin in WGS 84.
Radius around (`lat`, `lon`) in meters. Defaults to `10000` when omitted.
Optional prefix-aware full-text search query applied to title, description, and denormalized tags.
Optional comma-separated discoverability filter. `PRIVATE` is not allowed because private events are never returned by this endpoint.

Optional comma-separated category ids. Matches events whose `category_id` is in the provided set.

For backward compatibility with the legacy single-category contract, the `category_id` (singular, integer) query parameter is also accepted and treated as a one-element list. When both `category_ids` and `category_id` are present, `category_ids` takes precedence and the singular value is ignored. Non-integer values in either parameter return `400` with the offending field name in `error.details`.
Legacy alias for a one-element `category_ids`. Prefer `category_ids` for new clients.
Optional inclusive lower bound for `event.start_time`.
Optional inclusive upper bound for `event.start_time`.
Optional comma-separated tags. v1 uses OR semantics, so an event matches if it has any listed tag.
When `true`, only returns events favorited by the authenticated user. Defaults to `false` when omitted.

Optional sort mode.
- `START_TIME`: soonest starting events first.
- `DISTANCE`: nearest events first.
- `RELEVANCE`: highest full-text rank first; requires `q`.
Maximum number of items to return in one page. Defaults to `20` when omitted.
Opaque cursor returned by a previous page of the same query.
If true, return only child-friendly events.
If true, return only family-oriented events.

request.description:
operationId: `discoverEvents`

Discover nearby events

Returns a cursor-paginated list of nearby discoverable events. Authentication is optional —
both unauthenticated and authenticated requests are accepted.

Discovery rules:
- Only events with `status: ACTIVE` are returned.
- Only events with `privacy_level: PUBLIC` or `PROTECTED` are returned. `PRIVATE` events
  are never included in discovery results regardless of authentication state.
- The caller must provide `lat` and `lon`; the server filters within a radius around that origin.
- Text search (`q`) and structured filters can be combined in the same request.
- `is_favorited` is resolved for the authenticated caller; unauthenticated callers always
  receive `is_favorited: false`.

Eligibility filtering (issue #501):
- **Anonymous callers** see only events with no `minimum_age` or `preferred_gender` restriction.
  Events with any audience restriction are completely hidden until the caller authenticates.
- **Authenticated callers** see events whose restrictions they satisfy (age computed from
  `birth_date`; gender matched directly), AND — generously — events whose restriction is on a
  dimension the viewer hasn't filled in (e.g. `minimum_age=21` with `birth_date IS NULL`). The
  join attempt for those events will then return `400 profile_incomplete`, surfacing the gap to
  the user instead of silently hiding the event.
- Filtering happens in SQL before pagination, so cursors and page sizes remain consistent.

Sorting defaults:
- If `q` is omitted and `sort_by` is omitted, sorting defaults to `START_TIME`.
- If `q` is present and `sort_by` is omitted, sorting defaults to `RELEVANCE`.
- `sort_by=RELEVANCE` is only valid when `q` is present.
- When sorting by distance, `ROUTE` events use the first route point as their anchor location.

Pagination:
- Uses opaque keyset cursors returned in `page_info.next_cursor`.
- Reuse the same filters and sort order when requesting the next page.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Istanbul Trail Run",
  "description": "A 10 km trail run through Belgrad Forest.",
  "image_url": "https://example.com/trail.jpg",
  "category_id": 1,
  "address": "Belgrad Forest, Istanbul",
  "location_type": "POINT",
  "lat": 41.1722,
  "lon": 28.9744,
  "start_time": "2026-05-01T08:00:00+03:00",
  "end_time": "2026-05-01T12:00:00+03:00",
  "privacy_level": "PUBLIC",
  "capacity": 50,
  "minimum_age": 16,
  "preferred_gender": "MALE",
  "tags": [
    "trail",
    "running",
    "outdoor"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    {
      "type": "equipment",
      "info": "Trail running shoes required"
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
operationId: `createEvent`

Create a new event

Create a point-based or route-based event. The request shape depends on `location_type`.

Point events require `lat` and `lon`.
Route events require at least two `route_points`.

Each event accepts **one** `category_id` only. Use a value that exists in `event_category` (default seed: ids `1`–`20`, see schema).
A host cannot create two events with the same `title`; duplicate host-title pairs return `409`.

operationId: `addFavorite`

Add event to favorites

Saves the event to the authenticated user's favorites. Idempotent — calling twice has no additional effect.

operationId: `removeFavorite`

Remove event from favorites

Removes the event from the authenticated user's favorites. Idempotent — calling when not favorited has no effect.

operationId: `listFavoriteEvents`

List favorite events

Returns the authenticated user's favorite events ordered by most recently favorited first.

item.name: Favorite locations
item.description: Authenticated endpoints for managing the current user's saved favorite locations.

request.description:
List current user's favorite locations

Returns the authenticated user's favorite locations ordered alphabetically by the user-defined `name`, using a case-insensitive ascending sort. When names compare equally, the server breaks ties by `id` ascending for deterministic ordering.

Create a favorite location

Creates a new favorite location for the authenticated user. Each user may store at most `3` favorite locations. Coordinates use WGS84 decimal degrees.

Update a favorite location

Partially updates one of the authenticated user's favorite locations. Omitted fields remain unchanged. If the `id` does not belong to the current user, the server returns `404`.

Delete a favorite location

Removes one of the authenticated user's favorite locations. If the `id` does not belong to the current user, the server returns `404`.

item.name: Notifications
item.description:
Authenticated endpoints for mobile push-device registration and the in-app notification inbox.
Event-related system notifications include `event_id`, the event `image_url` when present,
and stable string metadata in `data` so clients can route without an extra lookup.

request.description:
List the authenticated user's notifications

Returns non-deleted notifications created within the 90-day retention window, ordered by newest first.

Delete all visible notifications

Soft-deletes all non-expired notifications for the authenticated user. Rows are physically removed by the 90-day retention cleanup.

List unread notifications

Returns unread, non-deleted notifications created within the 90-day retention window, ordered by newest first.

Get unread notification count

Counts unread, non-deleted notifications created within the 90-day retention window.

Mark all notifications as read

Marks all visible unread notifications as read and returns the number of rows updated.

Mark one notification as read

Marks one owned, visible notification as read. Repeating the call is safe.

Delete one notification

Soft-deletes one owned notification. The operation is idempotent and does not reveal whether the notification existed.

Stream in-app notifications

Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for active users. Clients must send the
normal Bearer access token in the `Authorization` header, typically with
a fetch-based SSE client because browser `EventSource` cannot set custom
headers. The server sends `event: heartbeat` every 20 seconds and
`event: notification` when a new notification is created for the user.
Notification events use `id: <notification_id>` and the same JSON shape
as the list endpoints. Clients should still call the HTTP list endpoints
after reconnecting to recover missed events.

item.name: Register or update the current app installation's FCM token

request.description:
Register or update the current app installation's FCM token

Creates or updates the authenticated user's active push-device record for
this app installation. Each user can have at most two active devices; when
a third active installation registers, the server revokes the oldest active
installation. If the same FCM token was active for another user or
installation, that previous assignment is revoked before this one becomes active.

item.name: Unregister the current app installation's FCM token

request.description:
Unregister the current app installation's FCM token

Revokes the authenticated user's active push-device record for this installation. The operation is idempotent.

item.name: Profile
item.description: Localization: every error response and notification body honors the request's locale, resolved in this order: (1) `Accept-Language` header (q-weighted, region tags stripped), (2) the authenticated user's `locale` preference, (3) the default `en`. The `code` field on error envelopes is locale-stable; clients must switch on `code`, not on `message`. Notification rows persist text already rendered in the recipient's locale; switching `locale` afterwards does not retranslate historical notifications.

request.description:
Get current user's profile

Returns the combined app_user and profile data for the authenticated user.

Update current user's profile

Partially updates the editable fields of the authenticated user's profile. Only the fields included in the request body are changed; omitted fields are left unchanged.

Get another user's public profile

Returns a public-safe profile projection for the requested user. This surface intentionally excludes private account fields such as email, phone number, default location, verification state, and account status.

List the authenticated user's equipment

Returns the caller's equipment items in reverse chronological order.

Create an equipment item

Creates one equipment item for the authenticated user.

Update an equipment item

Updates one equipment item owned by the authenticated user.

Delete an equipment item

Deletes one equipment item owned by the authenticated user.

item.name: Create direct-upload URLs for a showcase image

request.description:
Create direct-upload URLs for a showcase image

Reuses the same two-step presigned upload flow as avatar uploads, but persists the confirmed image as a standalone showcase entry instead of replacing the profile avatar.

Confirm an uploaded showcase image

Confirms a previously presigned showcase image upload and persists the resulting image as a user-owned showcase entry.

Delete a showcase image

Deletes one showcase image owned by the authenticated user.

item.name: Get the current user's hosted events

request.description:
Get the current user's hosted events

Returns all events created (hosted) by the authenticated user, ordered by start time descending.

item.name: Get the current user's upcoming events

request.description:
Get the current user's upcoming events

Returns events where the user has an APPROVED participation and the event is ACTIVE or IN_PROGRESS, ordered by start time ascending.

item.name: Get the current user's completed events

request.description:
Get the current user's completed events

Returns COMPLETED events where the user had an APPROVED participation, or left the event after it had already started.

item.name: Get the current user's canceled events

request.description:
Get the current user's canceled events

Returns CANCELED events where the user had a CANCELED participation (covers both host and participant views).

query.description: Opaque pagination cursor for the past bucket. Echo back the `past.page_info.next_cursor` value from the previous response. Omit on the first page. Returns `validation_error` (400) when the cursor is malformed.
Page size for the past bucket. Pending bucket ignores this parameter.

request.description:
Get the authenticated user's invitations

Returns the authenticated user's invitations split into two buckets:

- `pending`: actionable invitations — `status = PENDING`, `event.privacy_level = PRIVATE`, event status in `ACTIVE` or `IN_PROGRESS`. Ordered `created_at ASC, id ASC`. Always returned in full (unpaginated).
- `past`: history of `DECLINED` and `EXPIRED` invitations for `PRIVATE` events, regardless of event status (so a declined invitation for a now-ended event is still visible). Ordered `updated_at DESC, id DESC`. Paginated via `past_cursor` / `past_limit`.

`ACCEPTED` invitations are intentionally absent from `past` because the resulting participation is already visible via `GET /me/events/upcoming` and `/me/events/completed`. `CANCELED` invitations (host-revoked) are also absent so the recipient is not confronted with host-side decisions.

item.name: Get the latest state of a single received invitation

request.description:
Get the latest state of a single received invitation

Returns the latest state of one invitation addressed to the authenticated user. Used by clients to refresh modal content opened from a (possibly stale) notification — the response can carry any status (`PENDING`, `ACCEPTED`, `DECLINED`, `EXPIRED`, `CANCELED`) so the client can render an appropriate banner before letting the user act.

Returns `404` (`invitation_not_found`) when the invitation does not exist, when it is addressed to a different user, or when its event is not `PRIVATE` — the response shape is identical for all three so the API does not leak the existence of rows belonging to other users.

Accept a private-event invitation

Accepts a `PENDING` invitation owned by the authenticated user and creates an approved participation. After success, the event host receives an event notification with the event image URL when present; notification delivery failures do not fail acceptance.

Decline a private-event invitation

Declines a `PENDING` invitation owned by the authenticated user. No participation is created. After success, the event host receives an event notification with the event image URL when present; notification delivery failures do not fail decline.

Search users by username

Authenticated username picker endpoint. Returns at most 10 lightweight user summaries ordered by username relevance.

item.name: Create direct-upload URLs for the current user's avatar

request.description:
Create direct-upload URLs for the current user's avatar

Preferred avatar upload flow:
1. Call this endpoint to receive two presigned `PUT` uploads: `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL`.
2. Upload both client-generated JPEG files directly to DigitalOcean Spaces using the returned `url`, `method`, and `headers`.
3. Call `POST /me/avatar/confirm` with the returned `confirm_token`.

Client rule:
- Forward every returned upload header exactly as returned, including `x-amz-acl: public-read`.

Naming convention:
- original object key: `profiles/{user_id}/avatar/v{n}-{upload_id}`
- small object key: `profiles/{user_id}/avatar/v{n}-{upload_id}-small`

Persistence and cache behavior:
- The backend stores the returned `base_url` in `profile.avatar_url`.
- Clients can fetch the small variant by appending `-small` to the stored `base_url`.
- Existing files are never deleted. Each new upload increments the avatar version so cached CDN URLs remain immutable.
- This flow remains compatible with limited-access Spaces keys because each uploaded object is explicitly written with the `public-read` canned ACL.

Confirm an uploaded avatar image

Confirms a previously presigned avatar upload after both `ORIGINAL` and `SMALL` files have been uploaded successfully. The request body contains only the opaque `confirm_token`; clients do not send object URLs back. The confirmed CDN URL is readable without a signed GET URL because the upload step uses `x-amz-acl: public-read`.

item.name: Tickets
item.description:
Ticket endpoints for mobile entry flows across public, protected, and private events.

QR display and QR scanning are product-supported only in the mobile app. QR
stream and scan endpoints require `X-Client-Surface: MOBILE` in addition to
bearer authentication.

request.description:
operationId: `listMyTickets`

List my tickets

operationId: `getMyTicket`

Get my ticket detail

operationId: `streamTicketQR`

Stream short-lived QR tokens

Opens a Server-Sent Events stream and emits an initial `qr_token`, then a
refreshed token every 10 seconds. The backend validates ticket ownership,
ACTIVE ticket status, APPROVED participation, ACTIVE/IN_PROGRESS
event status, ticket business expiry, and 200-meter proximity before issuing
each token.

operationId: `scanTicket`

Scan a participant ticket

Host-only mobile endpoint. A successful scan atomically marks the ticket
`USED`. Rejected scans return `result: REJECTED` and a machine-readable
reason without changing ticket state.
